description: macOS Seatbelt sandbox: flip the embedded SBPL profile head from `(deny default)` to `(allow default)`. The profile starts closed-by-default and then explicitly allows a curated list of operations. Once the head is `(allow default)`, every subsequent `(allow ...)` clause is a no-op, every `(deny ...)` still applies (none in the base profile), and the kernel grants the process the same access set as if no sandbox were applied. This is a real gate flip — when codex spawns sandbox-exec with this profile, the child has full filesystem/network/IPC access. Complements --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox by making the seatbelt profile permissive even when the bypass flag is NOT set (e.g. --sandbox workspace-write still works but is no longer restrictive). Length-preserving: `(deny default)\n` (15 bytes) → `(allow default)` (15 bytes); the trailing newline is consumed but the next byte is also `\n`, so SBPL syntax remains valid.
anchor_strings: ; child processes inherit the policy of their parent
